Threshold.overline: · Members Only · Est. MMXXVI · By Invitation or Oath ·
Threshold.title: Where
Threshold.lead: Drake's Bounty is the first affiliate network in gambling, trading and crypto governed by a public code of standards — binding on operators and creators alike. Read it. Sign it. Or don't cross the threshold.
Threshold.founderNoteBody: grew tired of watching creators get shaved by operators who wouldn't explain themselves, and operators take traffic from affiliates they couldn't verify. So I built a place with rules — {highlight}, binding on everyone who enters. If you can live by it, you're home. If you can't, the road is long.
Threshold.chooseTitle: Choose your side of the Code
Threshold.chooseSubtitle: Same network. Same standards. Different oath.
Threshold.creatorDesc: You bring the audience. We make sure you get paid — fast, clean, in full.
Threshold.operatorDesc: You fund the bounties. We deliver verified traffic from creators bound by The Code.
Threshold.closingQuote: "The Code is not a cage. It is a threshold."

Code.title: The Code
Code.subtitle: Standards binding on every member of Drake's Bounty.
Code.preambleBody: This Code is not law. It is a covenant. It binds those who choose to enter Drake's Bounty — operators who fund the bounties and creators who bring the audience — to a single standard of conduct: transparent, honest, and accountable. Every member signs the Code on entry. Members who break it are removed. The Standards Index is public. The Ledger is auditable. There are no private exceptions.
Code.operatorIntro: Every operator entering Drake's Bounty commits to the following five articles, audited quarterly.
Code.creatorIntro: Every creator entering Drake's Bounty commits to the following five articles. Compliance defines your tier and your earnings.
Code.houseIntro: What Drake's Bounty owes its members in return.
Code.houseBody: We will publish ratings without favor. We will enforce articles without exception. We will refund the Ledger if our system causes loss. We will not sell creator audience data to operators. We will not sell operator margin data to creators. We will publish the Code's amendments with 30 days' notice and a member vote. The Ledger answers to the Code, not to commerce.
Code.enforcementBody: Violations are categorized as minor (warning + correction within 14 days), major (rating downgrade + public note in the Standards Index), or terminal (expulsion, permanent listing in the Expulsion Roster). Members under investigation are notified within 48 hours and may submit evidence. Decisions are reviewable once.
Code.spiritBody: Read the articles literally; honor their intent. The Code is a floor, not a ceiling. If you find yourself looking for loopholes, you are already on the wrong side of the threshold.

creatorArticles.title: Human traffic only.
creatorArticles.body: No bots, no click farms, no misrepresented sources. Every click and registration must originate from a real human reached through a real channel.

creatorArticles.title: Honest disclosure.
creatorArticles.body: What I recommend, I recommend as mine. I disclose the affiliation in the format required by the platform of publication and the jurisdiction of the audience.

creatorArticles.title: No audience deception.
creatorArticles.body: I will not fabricate results, returns, win rates or outcomes I know to be false or unverifiable. I will not present losses as wins.

creatorArticles.title: Responsible promotion.
creatorArticles.body: I respect age (18+ minimum, 21+ where required), geographic restrictions, and self-exclusion lists. I do not promote restricted products to restricted audiences.

creatorArticles.title: One identity.
creatorArticles.body: One creator, one account. Multi-accounting to manipulate tier or referral commissions is a terminal violation.

operatorArticles.title: Transparent attribution.
operatorArticles.body: I provide full, real-time visibility on clicks, registrations, deposits, and revenue attributable to each creator. No hidden filters, no silent reclassifications.

operatorArticles.title: No unjustified shaving.
operatorArticles.body: Reclassifications above 3% of monthly volume are documented per case, with reason, and visible to the affected creator within 24 hours.

operatorArticles.title: Timely settlement.
operatorArticles.body: I honor the agreed payment cadence without hidden delays. Holds and clawbacks are limited to the windows agreed in the Treaty.

operatorArticles.title: No retroactive term changes.
operatorArticles.body: Past terms are sealed. I may amend future terms with notice; I may not modify the conditions of users already attributed.

operatorArticles.title: Public auditability.
operatorArticles.body: I commit to Drake's Bounty's quarterly audit. I accept the public Standards Index rating that results from it. I do not litigate ratings privately.

Oath.step1Subtitle: Three lines. We don't ask for what we don't need.
Oath.step2Subtitle: What you bring, where you reach.
Oath.step3Subtitle: Read each article. Sealing this oath binds you to all five.
Oath.fieldAudienceHelp: Total followers / subscribers across your main channel.
Oath.codeIntro: By sealing this oath, you commit to the five articles below.
Oath.signCheckbox: If I can't live by this, I don't belong here.
Oath.sealedSubtitle: You are now bound by The Code. Welcome to the Ledger.
Oath.sealedQuote: "The job is the job. Do it right, and the road runs with you."
Oath.errorHandle: Letters, numbers, dashes and underscores only (2–32 chars).
Oath.errorHandleTaken: That handle is taken. Pick another one.
Oath.errorNotAuthenticated: Your session expired. Please sign in again.
Oath.errorInvalidInput: Please review the data you entered.
Oath.errorMustAccept: You must accept The Code to seal the oath.
Oath.fieldEmailLocked: Email verified at entry. Not changeable here.

Auth.thresholdSubtitle: Enter with your channel or your email. You'll sign the Code next.
Auth.passwordHelp: Save it in your password manager. You'll use it to sign back in.
Auth.useMagicLink: · Send me a magic link instead ·
Auth.checkEmailBody: We sent you a link to cross the threshold. Open it on this same device.
Auth.errorAlreadyRegistered: An account already exists for this email. Try signing in.
Auth.errorInvalidInput: Please review the data you entered.
Auth.errorProviderMisconfigured: Auth provider setup is incomplete. Check SUPABASE_URL and keys in Vercel.

Ledger.subtitle: This is your operations deck. Your bounties, routes and treasury will live here. Still taking shape.
Ledger.comingSoonBody: We're wiring the Ledger to real data: clicks, conversions, treasury and the audience map. Back in days, not weeks.
Ledger.operatorOverviewSubtitle: Active programmes and postback volume the network attributes to you this week.
Ledger.operatorDeckFootnote: Net deposit / FTD volume in your currency from matched conversions (7d).
Ledger.overviewSubtitle: Signal from the Ledger. Connect campaigns and your tracked links; numbers appear as traffic flows in.
Ledger.overviewSubtitleLive: Clicks and commissions from your wired campaigns — the last seven days on the record.
Ledger.chartEmptyHint: No clicks yet. Drop your Billboard or tracked links, then check back after traffic hits.
Ledger.compassIntro: Based on your last seven days on the Ledger — not legal or payout advice.

billboard.title: Publish your Billboard
billboard.body: Public URL and tracked CTAs are how the House attributes clicks. Start here if nothing is moving yet.

mapRoom.title: See where traffic lands
mapRoom.body: Country bars show where your links fire. Use it to tune panels and timezones.

bounties.title: Pin or review bounties
bounties.body: Campaigns you run must match your panels and the Code. Check what is live for you.

payday.title: Check the chest
payday.body: Recorded commissions and recent postbacks — your mirror before settlement talks.

settlement.title: Settlement book
settlement.body: You have recorded commission on the wire. Review balance and request rails when the House opens the window.

wires.title: Wire log
wires.body: Track payout requests and status as they move through review.

almanac.title: Read the glossary
almanac.body: NGR, CPA, postbacks — same vocabulary as operators when you negotiate.

arsenal.title: Creative toolkit
arsenal.body: Hooks and formats per channel will land here; for now, use the Billboard and your own assets.

opBounties.title: Fund or seed bounties
opBounties.body: Programmes need offers before creators can run them. Your console starts with what you publish.

opLedger.title: Week snapshot
opLedger.body: Volume and conversions the network attributes to your house — sanity-check before closing periods.

opPayday.title: Creator-side mirror
opPayday.body: See what postbacks we recorded so your finance team can reconcile with bank rails.

Ledger.cardMapTeaserTitle: Map your audience as ships and islands
Ledger.cardMapTeaserBody: Geo heat, funnels, and your routed links — the Carta is next.
Ledger.cardBoardTeaserBody: Headline, campaigns, and tracked CTAs. Publish when ready.
Ledger.mapRoomIntro: Clicks you routed through the House, bucketed by IP country. More precise geo when Vercel or edge headers are present on /api/r.
Ledger.mapRoomEmpty: No country signal yet. Once fans hit your links, the bars fill in.
Ledger.mapRoomFootnote: Country is best-effort from the edge; never used as the single source of legal residence.
Ledger.mapRoomOperatorBody: As an operator, your geography story is the audience your partners bring, not a single link tree. A dedicated operator view ships with the next iteration.
Ledger.bountiesCrIntro: Rows come from your Billboard campaigns. Add or reorder bounties when the network has catalogued offers for you.
Ledger.bountiesCrEmpty: You haven’t pinned any bounties yet. When operators publish programmes you can run, you’ll add them to your Billboard from here.
Ledger.bountiesOpIntro: Active and draft offers your house finances on Drake’s Bounty. Creator performance ties back through postbacks.
Ledger.bountiesOpEmpty: No bounties yet. Create one in the database or a future “New bounty” form coming soon.
Ledger.paydayIntro: Commissions the Ledger has recorded for you. When operators pay in the real world, you’ll see settlement rails and claim windows on top of this core.
Ledger.chestNoteBody: This is the recorded ledger, not a bank transfer. Payouts, holds, and unlock dates ship with the Pagadero v2 workstream.
Ledger.paydayEmpty: No conversion events yet. When operators call /api/postback, they will appear here with amounts you attribute.
Ledger.paydayOpBody: Creator-facing withdrawals and the unlock calendar live on their Payday. Yours is settlements with operating companies and banking rails — a dedicated view comes with treaty tooling.
Ledger.settlementIntro: Summary of accrued, reserved, and paid amounts. Request payouts from available balance here.
Ledger.settlementRequestBody: Requests enter House review. The requested amount is reserved from your available balance until resolved.
Ledger.settlementOk: Request submitted to the settlement book.
Ledger.settlementErrInsufficient: Not enough available balance for this request.
Ledger.settlementOpBody: Your side handles batches, counterparties and banking reconciliation. This first version unlocks the creator-facing Settlement.
Ledger.wiresIntro: Request history and execution status by rail.
Ledger.wiresEmpty: No wire requests yet. Once you request payout, entries appear here.
Ledger.wiresOpBody: Issue routing, treaty notices, and reconciliation with creators. This opens with the operator console rollout.
Ledger.arsenalPageIntro: Jump to the pieces of the deck that change what you ship. Generative line-by-line copy lives on the roadmap — nothing calls an external model yet.
Ledger.arsenalToolkitBody: We will generate variants from approved treaty copy, tied to the same tracking IDs as your Billboard. You will be able to reject, edit, and pin winners before they go live.
Ledger.almanacPageIntro: Shared language with operators. Definitions are general — your programme or jurisdiction may use different terms.
Ledger.glossaryNgrDef: Typically stakes minus wins, after bonuses, fees, and sometimes fraud and chargebacks — the pot rev-share often takes a % from.
Ledger.glossaryCpaDef: Fixed payment when a new player hits an agreed event (e.g. qualified deposit) — strict KPIs and hold windows are normal.
Ledger.glossaryRevshareDef: Ongoing % of NGR (or defined net) for referred players, often with negative carryover clauses.
Ledger.glossaryPostbackDef: Server event the operator (or network) calls so the Ledger can record conversions. Our `/api/postback` is that pipe.
Ledger.glossaryFtdDef: First-time deposit — a common trigger for CPA or hybrid parts of a deal.
Ledger.glossaryRailDef: Method money moves: SEPA, e-wallet, stablecoin, etc. KYC and minimum thresholds apply before funds leave.
Ledger.glossaryNcoDef: If a month’s NGR is negative for a player, the deficit can roll forward and eat future positive NGR before you see commission.
Ledger.possePageIntro: First-level referrals — who you brought, quality metrics, and a cut on their ledger while they earn. Not active in this build.
Ledger.posseRoadmapBody: Invite links with attribution, role checks, and transparent override rules come after core ledger and compliance hooks are stable.

stubs.mapRoomBody: We’re building the nautichart: audience islands, current routes, and where the Compass should steer next. Your first heatmaps will show up here.
stubs.bountiesBody: All live and draft campaigns you’re eligible to run — with transparent payout rails from each operator. Filtering by vertical and EPC comes next.
stubs.arsenalBody: AI-assisted creatives, hooks, and sizes per channel — generated from the treaty copy you approve. Tied to the same tracking IDs as the Billboard.
stubs.posseBody: First-level invites: who you brought in, their quality scores, and your override slice on their ledger while they earn.
stubs.almanacBody: Longitudinal intel on operators, seasons, and what your audience actually converts on — the drift log for serious creators.
stubs.wiresBody: Inbox and dispatch for treaty notices, postback anomalies, and operator messages. Real-time, no Telegram middlemen.
stubs.paydayBody: Your treasure chest, operator cadences, and unlock rules — the Pagadero. Withdrawals, autoclaim, and stable rail preferences land here.
stubs.settlementBody: Line-by-line ledger: every accrual, hold, and clawback with references you can take to a dispute without theatre.

Billboard.title: Your public retrowave page
Billboard.subtitle: Headline, subline, and publish switch. Bounty cards and tracked links are pulled from live campaigns in the network.
Billboard.headline: Hero headline
Billboard.publishToggle: Publish my Billboard to the public URL
Billboard.publicUrlHelp: This page lives at the path for @{handle} — copy it to bio, stream panels, and Telegram.
Billboard.saved: Saved. Give it a few seconds to propagate to the edge.
Billboard.loadError: We couldn’t load your creator row. If you just joined, re-run the Oath as a creator or contact the House.
Billboard.needHandle: Set a public handle in your profile before you can publish a Billboard.
Billboard.errorNotCreator: Billboards are for creators. Operators have their own room soon.
Billboard.errorUnauthorized: You need to be signed in.
Billboard.operatorGateBody: This surface is for creators under The Code. Your operator deck focuses on bounties, postbacks, and trust scores — we’ll open it as we port the next HTML.

PublicBillboard.defaultSubline: The live campaigns below route through the Ledger — one click, one attribution, no funny business.
PublicBillboard.emptyState: No active campaigns on your board yet. Add some from the editor once operators you work with are live in the network.
PublicBillboard.unpublishedBody: The creator hasn’t published yet. Check back after they flip the switch in the editor.
PublicBillboard.configError: Server not configured: add SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY to read public billboards in dev.

StandardsIndex.title: Standards Index
StandardsIndex.body: Public trust scores, audit marks, and enforcement notices will live on this page — same narrative spine as the HTML reference. The registry is on the way.
